color_semantics.TEAL: quantum-state preparation uncertainty / Robertson bound / σ_x·σ_p ≥ ℏ/2
color_semantics.CRIMSON: classical measurement-disturbance story / photon-kick / balloon analogy (wrong picture)

metadata.note: Card 08 — score 8/10 — transform move — exclusions: no Cauchy–Schwarz proof, no commutator algebra, no Ozawa inequality beyond one contrast sentence

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. You've heard the story. To see an electron you need a photon. That photon

beats.prompt: The balloon analogy. Bump it to find it, bumping moves it. Seems right. Gives the right order-of-magnitude. But it is the wrong picture.
beats.label: The balloon analogy. Bump it to find it, bumping moves it. Seems right. Gives the right order-of-mag

beats.prompt: The question: if the uncertainty principle isn't about the kick, what is it actually about?
beats.label: The question: if the uncertainty principle isn't about the kick, what is it actually about?

beats.prompt: Here is what Robertson actually proved in 1929. A Gaussian state has width σ_x in position and width σ_p in momentum. Squeeze one — the other must grow.
beats.label: Here is what Robertson actually proved in 1929. A Gaussian state has width σ_x in position and width

beats.prompt: The Robertson bound: σ_x times σ_p is always at least ℏ over 2. This is a property of the wavefunction itself — no apparatus, no kick, no observer.
beats.label: The Robertson bound: σ_x times σ_p is always at least ℏ over 2. This is a property of the wavefuncti

beats.prompt: Position and momentum don't commute: x-hat times p-hat minus p-hat times x-hat equals i-ℏ. That commutator is why the two widths can't both be zero simultaneous
beats.label: Position and momentum don't commute: x-hat times p-hat minus p-hat times x-hat equals i-ℏ. That comm

beats.prompt: The harmonic oscillator ground state saturates the bound exactly: σ_x times σ_p equals ℏ over 2. Every excited state exceeds it. The ground state is the tightes
beats.label: The harmonic oscillator ground state saturates the bound exactly: σ_x times σ_p equals ℏ over 2. Eve

beats.prompt: This is why you cannot even prepare a particle with simultaneously sharp position and momentum. Not measurement, not disturbance — preparation. The state forbid
beats.label: This is why you cannot even prepare a particle with simultaneously sharp position and momentum. Not

beats.prompt: Two different inequalities, often confused. Robertson 1929: preparation uncertainty — the state itself. Ozawa 2003: measurement-disturbance — apparatus noise pl
beats.label: Two different inequalities, often confused. Robertson 1929: preparation uncertainty — the state itse

beats.prompt: Ground state ψ₀ proportional to e to the minus alpha x-squared over 2. Computing directly: σ_x equals root ℏ over 2m-omega; σ_p equals root ℏ-m-omega over 2. Th
beats.label: Ground state ψ₀ proportional to e to the minus alpha x-squared over 2. Computing directly: σ_x equal

beats.prompt: The question was: what is the uncertainty principle actually about? The answer: it is a lower bound on the product of widths of any quantum state — written befo
beats.label: The question was: what is the uncertainty principle actually about? The answer: it is a lower bound
